title: Generated CLI next stage

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Plan and implement the next generated-CLI stage tracked by GitHub #880 after the Python generated package relocation.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not collapse all generated-CLI work into one broad rewrite. Pick one bounded stage with concrete acceptance, preserve the Python generated package behavior, and keep reusable generated-CLI code under the intended generated hierarchy.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: The exact first bounded stage, likely TypeScript CLI usability/hardening or shared generated CLI contract cleanup, after inspecting current generated surfaces and issue context.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A correct next stage requires changing the generated-CLI product direction, publishing strategy, or multi-language hierarchy agreed with the user.
canonical_core.next_action: Shape GitHub #880 into the first bounded generated-CLI implementation slice, then implement and prove that slice.
completion_criteria: GitHub #880 is decomposed into a bounded first generated-CLI implementation stage.
The selected stage is implemented without regressing the Python generated package path.
Tests and maintainer proof demonstrate the generated-CLI stage is usable or the remaining blocker is explicitly routed.

goal: Advance generated CLIs beyond the Python relocation by selecting and implementing the next bounded stage.
non_goals: Do not rewrite every generated CLI surface in one slice.
Do not move unique repo source into generated directories before generated CLIs have truly landed.

intent.outcome: Plan and implement the next generated-CLI stage tracked by GitHub #880 after the Python generated package relocation.
intent.constraints: Keep the slice bounded; preserve Python generated package behavior and intended generated hierarchy.
intent.latitude: Choose the first bounded generated-CLI stage after inspecting current generated surfaces and issue context.
intent.escalation: Escalate if the stage requires changing generated-CLI product direction, publishing strategy, or multi-language hierarchy.

execution.next_step: Shape GitHub #880 into the first bounded generated-CLI implementation slice, then implement and prove that slice.
execution.proof: Planning summary plus targeted generated-CLI/workspace tests for the selected slice.

invariants: One generated-CLI stage at a time.
Generated surfaces should remain clearly distinct from source unique to this repo.

planning_mode.halt_instruction: Continue only after shaping the next generated-CLI stage into a bounded implementation slice.
minimal_success_criteria: implementation scope and proof are bounded
Python generated package behavior remains protected
planning_mode.manual_tightening_policy: tighten the plan only enough to support the next bounded implementation slice

iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: continue the current milestone until the completion criteria are met

intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Plan and implement the next generated-CLI stage tracked by GitHub #880 after the Python generated package relocation.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Shape #880 into one bounded generated-CLI implementation slice before editing generated CLI surfaces.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Planning surfaces plus generated CLI sources/tests selected during #880 shaping.
execution_bounds.max changed files: Keep the first stage reviewable; decompose if it spans unrelated generated CLI languages or packaging surfaces.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run agentic-workspace summary --target . --format json --select planning_surface_health,warnings,warning_count; make test-workspace; make lint-workspace
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Any move of unique repo source into generated directories or a broad multi-language generated CLI rewrite.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated package modules, deferred relocation #661, or broad source hierarchy moves.
execution_bounds.manual JSON validation: Do not run ad hoc JSON validation loops; use summary or package checks.

stop_conditions.stop when: The selected generated-CLI stage is complete and proven, or the stage must be split.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: The next stage requires product-direction or hierarchy decisions beyond #880.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: The work turns into broad generated CLI replacement or unrelated package relocation.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: Selected proof cannot demonstrate generated-CLI usability.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, the promoted item, and the narrow files needed for the current implementation step.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Fill in execution bounds, touched paths, and validation before implementation starts.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Plan and implement the next generated-CLI stage tracked by GitHub #880 after the Python generated package relocation.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Keep the next stage bounded and preserve generated hierarchy decisions.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Choose the first implementation slice, touched paths, and proof commands once current generated CLI surfaces are inspected.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A better-looking fix changes generated CLI product direction or broad source hierarchy.

post_decomposition_delegation.decision rule: After this slice is bounded, decide whether direct work, read-only exploration, implementation handoff, validation handoff, or stronger review improves quality or saves tokens safely.
post_decomposition_delegation.required evidence: slice id, route, reason, quality risk, token-saving class, read-first refs, write scope, proof burden, stop conditions, and return contract

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Preserve the larger intended outcome separately from this bounded slice.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Keep the slice bounded while carrying any larger follow-on through explicit continuation fields.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Did this slice advance the declared larger outcome, or only complete the local task?

active_milestone.scope: Keep this execution thread bounded to the promoted TODO item.

immediate_next_action: Shape GitHub #880 into the first bounded generated-CLI implementation slice, then implement and prove that slice.
invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.
completion_criteria: GitHub #880 is decomposed into a bounded first generated-CLI implementation stage.
The selected stage is implemented without regressing the Python generated package path.
Tests and maintainer proof demonstrate the generated-CLI stage is usable or the remaining blocker is explicitly routed.

execution_run.what happened: next-stage planning scaffold created; implementation has not started
execution_run.next step: Shape GitHub #880 into the first bounded generated-CLI implementation slice, then implement and prove that slice.

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.

required_for: before any product or handoff file creation
control_gates.next_action: HALT: prep-only mode active. Create Planning state, run agentic-workspace summary --target . --profile compact --format json, then stop. Do not manually tighten or revalidate generated JSON unless summary reports a blocking Planning problem. Do not create product files, scaffolds, README, PLANNING_STATE, or handoff documentation.
